research_profile.style_preference: 英文和中文分别生成独立 Markdown；Paper Radar 默认展示英文；克制、具体、有判断力，避免新闻稿式写作

topic_threads.summary: This issue stayed in the fresh May 3-4 window and selected FlexSQL, Chart-FR1, and PV-VAE. The common move is to improve what a model inspects before answering or generating: database schemas/values/execution traces, chart OCR and local visual regions, and future video frames withheld from the encoder.
topic_threads.judgment: The useful pattern is not only external audit after the answer. Agents and generative models need inspection loops or predictive pressure before the answer: query the database while planning, focus visual evidence while reasoning, and force latents to predict future dynamics. This is a narrower and fresher continuation of the evidence-surface theme.

topic_threads.summary: This issue focused on where agent evidence should live once work leaves the chat window: Claw-Eval-Live grounds workflow-agent scores in live demand signals and observable service/workspace artifacts, ObjectGraph moves document evidence into a traversable typed file format, and CIRM makes reward-model shortcut signals explicit enough to intervene before they become DPO labels.
topic_threads.judgment: The common move is to stop treating the prompt as the evidence container. Stronger agent systems need external surfaces that can be refreshed, traversed, audited, or causally edited: benchmark releases, document formats, and reward-model activations.

topic_threads.theme: Checks before multimodal and agent systems drift
topic_threads.summary: This issue moved earlier in the failure chain: PRISM corrects SFT-induced distribution drift before multimodal RLVR, PhyCo exposes physical-property controls before generated video motion drifts from dynamics, FCMBench-Video preserves temporal document evidence before flattened OCR loses auditability, and LAD probes activation trajectories before multi-turn adversarial intent becomes an explicit harmful request.
topic_threads.judgment: The common pattern is not another final-answer benchmark. Each paper creates a pre-final check: distribution alignment, physical control variables, temporal evidence ledgers, or activation-path sensors. This is a useful direction because fluent final artifacts are increasingly easy to produce and increasingly poor as the only inspection point.

topic_threads.theme: Structured operating surfaces for reliable agents
topic_threads.summary: This issue shifted from scientific-agent interfaces to the concrete operating surfaces that keep agents correct: shared web-to-table workboards, step-level escalation monitors for computer-use agents, trace-native contamination diagnostics, and reward-head-centered interpretability for reward models.
topic_threads.judgment: The strongest pattern is that long-horizon agents need inspectable surfaces where state, routing, evidence, cost, and training signals can be checked locally. More context and stronger models help, but the interface determines whether errors can be localized and corrected.

topic_threads.summary: This issue moved from harder evaluation objects to the interfaces research agents need: method-evolution graphs, domain foundation-model calls, full-text literature discovery, and inspectable scientific-visualization workflow states.
topic_threads.judgment: The strongest pattern is that research agents fail when language is the only interface. Useful systems expose method lineage, specialist computation, evidence search, and workflow state as structured objects that can be queried or checked.

topic_threads.theme: Hard evidence for data and workflow agents
topic_threads.summary: This issue shifted from middle-layer architecture to harder evaluation objects: executable scientific environments, implicit predictive table tasks, exact GUI state labels, and competence-aware reward-hypothesis verification.
topic_threads.judgment: The strongest pattern is that agents are being evaluated against inspectable artifacts rather than fluent end states. This makes failures less flattering but more useful for training and deployment review.

topic_threads.theme: Explicit middle layers for agents and documents
topic_threads.summary: This issue connected a unified driving world model, latent physical reasoning for VLA post-training, manifold-aware interpretability, and staged OCR/retrieval for KYC extraction. The common move was to expose the representation between input and output and make it optimizable or inspectable.
topic_threads.judgment: The middle layer is no longer just a hidden state. In these papers it becomes the control surface, evidence boundary, or interpretability object, and that seems like a healthier direction than pure end-to-end scaling.

topic_threads.summary: Third issue focused on making long-horizon agent workspaces explicit and recoverable: synthetic user computers for productivity simulations, OS-level checkpoint/restore for sandboxes, RL-resistance model organisms, and verifiable image-text alignment in interleaved documents.
topic_threads.judgment: The frontier is shifting from final-answer evaluation toward full workspace state: files, processes, trajectories, and multimodal evidence bindings must be generated, preserved, stress-tested, and checked.

topic_threads.summary: First Paper Radar issue centered on making agent state inspectable: executable task/verifier pipelines, training-time world-model transition supervision, environment-aware process rewards, and optical evidence retrieval.
topic_threads.judgment: The strongest common pattern is not longer context alone, but explicit state objects that can be generated, checked, retrieved, or distilled.

topic_threads.summary: Second issue shifted from static verifier/state papers toward feedback loops and evidence trails: delayed real-world rewards, verifiable RAG traces, data-visualization workflows, and mechanistic probes of post-training damage.
topic_threads.judgment: The useful frontier is not only better agent scores, but loops where rewards, traces, user clarifications, and internal model changes can be audited.

open_questions: Can bilingual SQL/Python data-agent execution preserve auditability when the winning answer is produced procedurally and then transpiled back to SQL?
Can predictive video latent objectives incorporate actions and interventions so the latent state becomes useful for planning, not only generation?
Can focus traces in chart and document models be exposed as durable audit evidence after deployment rather than only serving as training-time reasoning tags?
Can data agents learn a calibrated stopping policy for schema/value exploration instead of relying mainly on larger test-time sample budgets?
Can shared workboards support cross-worker contradiction resolution rather than only parallel row filling?
Can GUI-agent event detectors transfer across products, slow services, and enterprise-specific UI states?
Can trace divergence trigger targeted validation before final-answer checking, with different policies for extraction, computation, and synthesis failures?
Can reward-model interpretability become a live RLHF alarm instead of a post-hoc notebook?
Can scientific workflow agents expose intermediate visual or computational states as first-class verification objects before final files are produced?
Can literature-search agents maintain durable hypothesis ledgers so failed constraints improve later search rather than wasting more turns?
Can scientific agents detect when a specialist foundation model is out of distribution or poorly calibrated before routing decisions through it?
Can method-evolution graphs be edited, audited, and versioned by domain experts without breaking downstream research-agent operators?
Can reward-hypothesis verification transfer from embodied control to language-agent traces, tool calls, and final-answer rewards?
Can exact GUI-state labels be attached to multi-step interactive rollouts without losing failure attribution?
Can table agents ask for missing historical data instead of forcing a prediction from whatever table is present?
Can automatically generated scientific verifiers be audited for hidden brittleness beyond agreement with gold scripts?
Will synthetic agent-task generators converge on reusable verifier patterns that transfer outside their benchmark harnesses?
Can world-model teachers expose reliability estimates for each generated transition instead of relying mainly on hard filtering?
Can data and document agents move from answer accuracy toward audit trails that survive deployment review?
How do optical-memory methods behave when one episode mixes tables, code diffs, screenshots, multilingual documents, and UI history?
Can live prediction environments attach calibrated uncertainty to delayed labels when outcomes are ambiguous or sources conflict?
Can post-training pipelines detect internal knowledge-access damage before it appears as deployment failures?
Can synthetic workspaces include enough everyday mess, stale files, broken references, and collaborator drift to avoid overfitting to curated file worlds?
Can checkpoint/restore systems preserve browser profiles, GUI state, remote services, GPU memory, and external databases as reliably as Linux sandbox state?
Can agentic RL pipelines detect strategic under-exploration without relying on visible chain-of-thought or narrowly engineered model organisms?
Can multimodal document agents expose image-text alignment confidence before multi-step reasoning begins?
Can world-query bridges expose uncertainty instead of only higher benchmark scores?
Can latent reasoning trajectories be audited or constrained the way outputs are?
Can concept manifolds become stable steering handles across models and layers?
Can staged OCR-plus-retrieval pipelines stay robust when OCR gets worse or layouts get messier?
Can PRISM-style pre-alignment replace perception/reasoning experts with tool-state or environment-state experts for data and GUI agents?
Can physical property maps scale from clean object interactions to cluttered manipulation scenes with hands, tools, occlusion, and multi-object contact?
Can document-video agents expose a durable evidence ledger that records what was read, when it was visible, and why it was trusted?
Can activation trajectory probes collect enough representative benign deployment data to avoid becoming another brittle classifier?
Can visual prompt-injection robustness be separated from recency bias in document-video workflows?
Can live workflow benchmarks expose enough failure evidence without turning semantic dimensions into another judge-model benchmark?
Can graph-native agent documents support cross-file edge resolution, versioning, and team authoring without becoming harder to maintain than Markdown plus retrieval?
Can reward-model interventions move beyond surface style features to deployment artifacts such as citation shape, JSON verbosity, unverified confidence, or clean-looking but wrong tool traces?

recent_digests.title_en: Agents That Look Before They Answer
recent_digests.title_zh: 先看清证据，再让模型回答
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected three fresh May 3-4 open arXiv HTML papers after SkillHub setup, memory deduplication, local arXiv/API search, and academic/lab-blog subagent cross-checks. The screened 24-hour pool included MolmoAct2, orchestration-trace RL, FlexSQL, latent MLLM reasoning, AcademiClaw, ARA, AutoFocus, long-horizon training, DataClaw, and PhysicianBench. Chose a coherent inspection-before-answering theme and rewrote dense numeric tables as Markdown while saving only core method/analysis figures locally under images/paper-radar/2026-05-05-1128.

recent_digests.title_en: Evidence Surfaces for Agents Outside the Prompt
recent_digests.title_zh: 把证据从提示词里移出来
recent_digests.selection_note: May 1-3 target-topic arXiv was sparse, so the run expanded to Apr 30 after deduplicating against all prior Paper Radar items. Selected three open arXiv HTML papers with enough method detail and figures for deep reading: a live workflow-agent benchmark, a graph-native document format, and a reward-model causal intervention paper. Dense evidence tables were rewritten as Markdown; method diagrams and plots were saved locally under images/paper-radar/2026-05-04-0215.

recent_digests.title_zh: 在漂移变成答案之前加检查
recent_digests.selection_note: May 1-3 target-topic arXiv CS submissions were effectively empty, so the issue expanded to the newest Apr 30 fallback window. Selected four open arXiv HTML papers after memory deduplication, local arXiv/API search, and academic/community/lab-blog subagent cross-checks. The issue avoids another operating-surface/live-benchmark digest by focusing on checks before drift becomes a final artifact. Dense result tables were rewritten as Markdown rather than screenshotting ugly table crops.

recent_digests.title_en: Operating Surfaces for Agents That Need to Stay Correct
recent_digests.title_zh: 让智能体有可操作的结构化表面

The issue avoids another scientific-interface or benchmark-only digest by focusing on structured operating surfaces: workboards, event monitors, traces, and reward-head axes.

recent_digests.title_en: Hard Evidence for Data and Workflow Agents
recent_digests.title_zh: 让数据与界面智能体面对硬证据
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four fresh Apr 30 open arXiv papers after academic, Chinese-media, community, and lab-blog cross-checks. The issue avoided repeating the previous explicit-middle-layer theme by focusing on evaluation objects that harden data and workflow-agent evidence.

recent_digests.title_en: Why Agents Need an Explicit Middle Layer
recent_digests.title_zh: 为什么智能体需要显式中间层
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four open-access papers with readable HTML figures and, where useful, local table screenshots. The issue avoided repeating the previous verifier/workspace loop by centering explicit intermediate state across world models, latent RL, interpretability, and document extraction.

recent_digests.title_zh: 让长程智能体拥有可回放工作区
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four newest Apr 30 open arXiv papers after source-specialized candidate collection. Favored a coherent stack around workspaces, sandbox state, RL training failure modes, and multimodal evidence alignment over another broad GUI/data-agent benchmark issue.

recent_digests.title_zh: 从闭环反馈到可审计工作流
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four recent open arXiv papers with stable HTML figures and enough full-text detail for close reading. Deferred official model reports, medical-agent reports, and robotics world-model papers to avoid a diffuse issue.

recent_digests.title_zh: 让长程智能体拥有可核验的状态
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four recent open arXiv papers with accessible HTML full text, method figures, and result tables. Deferred social/media-hot leads when they were older, survey-like, or less suitable for close reading this round.

observations: This run avoided another Apr 30 fallback issue by staying inside the fresh May 3-4 arXiv window and selecting three papers with open HTML full text.
The final theme shifted from post-answer evidence surfaces to pre-answer inspection loops: database exploration, visual focus, and predictive latent pressure.
Dense result tables were rewritten as Markdown, while method and analysis figures were saved locally; no table screenshots were used for tables better represented in text.
SkillHub CLI and requested skills installed successfully into repo-local skills/, but those untracked installation artifacts must remain excluded from final repo changes.

This run avoided another generic state/workboard issue by narrowing the theme to evidence surfaces outside the prompt: benchmark artifacts, document graph structure, and reward-model mediator activations.
The posts add more field setup before each paper, include core CIRM formulas, and rewrite central numeric evidence as Markdown tables instead of using hard-to-read table screenshots.
ObjectGraph SVG figures required local Chromium rendering because arXiv HTML exposed them as inline SVG; the final images are local PNGs under images/paper-radar/2026-05-04-0215.

SkillHub CLI installed successfully and the requested skills were installed, but the SkillHub arXiv wrapper lacked the Python arxiv dependency; final search/reading used primary arXiv API/HTML instead.
This run followed the user feedback by adding field introductions, central formulas, and clean Markdown result tables instead of ugly recreated table screenshots.
The final theme shifted away from repeated workboard/live-benchmark framing and centered pre-final drift checks across training, world modeling, document evidence, and activation monitoring.
Figures were saved locally under images/paper-radar/2026-05-03-1138 with 300 DPI metadata; dense tables were rendered as Markdown and explained after the table.
SkillHub CLI and four requested skills installed successfully; SkillHub wrote installed skill files into repo-local skills/, and those untracked artifacts were removed before final verification.
This run incorporated the user's feedback by adding more field introductions before each paper and avoiding a title/abstract/evaluation template.
Figures were saved locally under images/paper-radar/2026-05-02-1604 and were used only when they directly supported a claim; no ugly recreated tables were used in the blog.
The reward-lens section included the core formula and the attribution-vs-patching distinction so the mechanism paper has technical depth rather than a vague summary.
SkillHub CLI and skills installed successfully, but the installed arxiv wrapper lacked its Python dependency; arXiv official API/HTML and primary source files were used instead.
This run used the newly installed SkillHub leads but relied on primary arXiv HTML full text for final paper reading and figures.
The final issue pivoted away from repeated hard-evidence/workspace framing and centered structured interfaces for scientific agents.
Each selected paper includes locally saved core figures under images/paper-radar/2026-05-02-1129; dense result tables were summarized without unshown table pointers.
English and Chinese posts are independent and use a research-note voice rather than a title/abstract/evaluation template.
This run used SkillHub setup as requested and then relied on primary open arXiv HTML/PDF sources for final selection.
The final theme avoided another world-model/middle-layer issue and instead centered verifiable data and GUI workflow evidence.
Core figures were saved locally under images/paper-radar/2026-05-02-0617; unshown tables were only paraphrased with source-locatable numbers.
English and Chinese posts were written as separate standalone posts with matching but natural structure.
The issue shifted away from the earlier verifier/closed-loop framing and instead centered explicit intermediate state across world models, latent control, geometry, and document extraction.
Local screenshots were created for the key comparison tables, which is safer than relying only on remote arXiv images.
The selected papers are still mostly arXiv-primary, but the mix is more diverse than another benchmark-only sweep.
The English and Chinese posts are independently readable and keep the same evidence without one being a stub of the other.
This run pivoted after academic subagent results surfaced stronger Apr 30 candidates, keeping the final issue aligned with the newest 24-hour window.
The issue avoided repeating prior verifier/closed-loop framing by using replayable workspaces as the through-line across training data, runtime state, RL safety, and document grounding.
Every figure discussed in the body is embedded as an image from arXiv HTML; unembedded tables are not referenced as standalone figures.
No local screenshots were needed, but reliance on remote arXiv images remains a link-stability risk.
No prior Paper Radar posts or seen records existed, so this run established the initial memory and dedup baseline.
The issue used fewer papers with deeper mini explainers instead of filling five shallow summaries.
Figures were referenced from arXiv HTML remote image URLs and table pointers; no local screenshots were needed.
The English and Chinese posts are independent rather than one being a direct stub of the other.
This run avoided repeating the previous verifiable-state framing and instead used closed-loop feedback and auditability as the theme.
Each selected paper includes at least two embedded figures rather than relying on unshown table pointers.
The search considered arXiv, Hugging Face Daily Papers, Chinese tech media, community leads, and official lab blogs, but final selection returned to open primary sources.
The issue still relies on remote arXiv HTML images; local screenshots were not needed because figure URLs were stable and directly embeddable.

improved: Used the self-evolution brief to avoid repeating the previous evidence-surface/workboard framing and instead selected papers around inspection before answering.
Kept the issue to three papers so each section could include an introduction, method steps, equations where relevant, source-backed tables, figure explanations, and caveats.
Used subagents for academic and lab-blog cross-checking, but kept final inclusion anchored to primary open arXiv HTML full text.
remaining_issues: PV-VAE's arXiv HTML did not expose reliable institution metadata, so the post conservatively marks institutions as not specified.
Subagent results for Chinese media and community sources arrived too late to influence final selection; their strongest leads should be treated as next-run candidates, not as verified covered papers.
The selected set is still arXiv-heavy; official lab reports were deferred because they were older, report-only, or less coherent with the fresh May 3-4 theme.
next_run_adjustments: Follow strict 24-hour leads not selected here: MolmoAct2, long-horizon training horizon length, DataClaw, AutoFocus, latent MLLM reasoning, and orchestration-trace RL.
If selecting a multi-agent training issue, prioritize credit assignment and stopping policies rather than another benchmark-only digest.
For document/chart papers, look for evidence traces that can be audited after deployment, not only during training-data construction.

improved: Converted the user feedback into concrete choices: field introductions, source-locatable formulas, local figure assets, and Markdown result tables for dense evidence.

remaining_issues: The freshest May 1-3 window still lacked enough target-topic source-native papers, so the issue relies on Apr 30 fallback papers surfaced through May 1 community/media attention.

Some ObjectGraph figure screenshots are rendered from inline SVG rather than PDF crops because the environment lacks PDF/image tooling such as ImageMagick or PIL; they are readable but not perfect archival crops.

next_run_adjustments: Check whether FlashRT, indirect prompt injection in the wild, xmemory/schema-grounded memory, SpecVQA, and multivector retrieval remain fresh enough for a document-agent safety/retrieval issue.

If selecting a world-model issue, prefer concrete empirical papers such as GS-Playground, S2VAE/topological encoding, or GSDrive over broad visual-generation surveys unless the issue is explicitly a roadmap digest.
For graph/document papers, look for cross-file linking, versioning, and human-authoring evidence instead of only token savings.
